seeded_stochastic_riverswim.exploration_mechanism: Optimistic Q-initialization (right-action Q = 1.0) + epsilon=0.5→0.05 over 20k steps
seeded_stochastic_riverswim.note: Pure epsilon-greedy (without optimistic init) fails completely on stochastic RiverSwim — agent never discovers reward at state 5. Optimistic initialization forces exploration against the current and is the standard approach for hard exploration in tabular average-reward RL.

open_boundaries: Jellybean World / foraging problems not yet integrated
GARNET / gymnasium continuing conversion needs full nonlinear (MLP) DifferentialSARSA
Nonlinear average-reward Horde/GVF integration on continuing gymnasium tasks
Average-reward actor-critic validated only on trivial bandits, not real continuing tasks
RiverSwim success requires optimistic initialization; pure epsilon-greedy is insufficient
note: Step 6 (2026-05-21): DifferentialSARSA proven on both deterministic chain (10/10 seeds, 0.9938 final reward) and stochastic RiverSwim (Strehl & Littman 2008) (10/10 seeds, 0.907 final reward, 97.5% right-action rate, 60x above random baseline). The stochastic benchmark uses optimistic initialization — the standard approach for hard exploration in tabular average-reward RL. Remaining open: nonlinear function approximation, gymnasium integration, Jellybean World.
